back_translation: But God remembered Noah and all the wild animals and livestock that were with him in the ark. God sent a wind blowing over the earth, and the waters began to subside.
The fountains of the deep and the gates of the sky were closed, and the rain from the heavens stopped.
The waters receded steadily from the earth, and after 150 days the waters had gone down.
On the 17th day of the 7th month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
The waters continued to recede until the 10th month. On the first day of the 10th month, the tops of the mountains became visible.
After 40 days, Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made.
He sent out a raven, and it kept flying back and forth until the waters had dried up from the earth.
Then Noah sent out a dove to see whether the waters had receded from the surface of the ground.

But the dove found no place to rest its feet, so it returned to Noah in the ark, because the waters were still covering the earth. Noah reached out his hand, took the dove, and brought it back into the ark.
Noah waited 7 more days and then sent out the dove from the ark again.
In the evening, the dove returned to Noah—and behold, it was carrying a freshly plucked olive leaf in its beak. So Noah knew that the waters had receded from the earth.
He waited 7 more days and sent out the dove again, but this time the dove did not return to him.

In the 601st year of Noah's life, on the first day of the first month, the waters had dried up from the earth. Noah opened the covering of the ark and looked—behold, the surface of the ground was already dry.
On the 27th day of the 2nd month, the earth was completely dry.
God said to Noah,
"Come out of the ark, you, your wife, your sons, and your sons' wives who are with you.
Bring out every living creature that is with you—birds, livestock, and every kind of crawling creature on the ground—so they can spread out on the earth, be fruitful, and multiply on the earth."
So Noah came out, along with his sons, his wife, and his sons' wives.
Every kind of animal, every kind of crawling creature, every kind of bird, and everything that moves on the earth came out of the ark, kind by kind.
Noah built an altar to the LORD, and taking from every kind of clean animal and clean bird, he offered burnt offerings on the altar.

When the LORD smelled the pleasing aroma, He resolved in His heart, "I will not curse the ground because of man again, even though the inclination of man's heart is only evil from his youth. And I will not destroy all living creatures again as I have done.
As long as the earth endures, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night shall not cease."
